expectations.text: The output contains the heading '## n8n Workflow Check'
expectations.evidence: Output starts with '## n8n Workflow Check'

expectations.text: The output identifies both nodes as having the wrong 'nodes-base.' prefix (should be 'n8n-nodes-base.')
expectations.evidence: Error 1 grouped: 'Telegram Trigger, Send Reply â€” Wrong type prefix: both nodes use nodes-base. instead of n8n-nodes-base.'

expectations.text: The output classifies the incorrect nodeType prefix as an Error, not a Warning
The output identifies chatId on 'Send Reply' as a plain string instead of an __rl object
expectations.evidence: Error 2: 'Send Reply â€” chatId must use __rl object format, not a plain string'

expectations.text: The output classifies the plain-string chatId as an Error
The output describes or shows the correct __rl format with __rl:true, value, and mode fields
The output reports the typeVersion 1.1 mismatch on Telegram Trigger (expected 1.2)
expectations.evidence: Warning 1: 'Telegram Trigger â€” typeVersion is 1.1, expected 1.2'

expectations.text: The output classifies the typeVersion mismatch as a Warning (not an Error)
The output reports the misspelled credential key 'telegramAPI' (should be 'telegramApi')
expectations.evidence: Warning 2: 'Telegram Trigger â€” Credential key "telegramAPI" is incorrectly capitalized'

expectations.text: The output offers to apply fixes after presenting the report
expectations.evidence: Ends with 'Want me to apply these fixes directly to the workflow?'

expectations.text: The output identifies exactly 4 issues (not more, not fewer)
The output does NOT flag the connections object as having any issues
expectations.evidence: 'Connection structure â€” linear chain wired correctly' listed under Clean

expectations.text: The output does NOT flag the 'updates: [message]' parameter as wrong
expectations.evidence: No mention of the updates parameter as an issue
